_synthetic_notice: SYNTHETIC — fabricated for demonstration only. No real patient data.

admission.primary_dx: Acute ischemic stroke, right middle cerebral artery territory

current.note: BP well controlled on new antihypertensive regimen. Neurologically stable.

medications.name: Aspirin
medications.indication: Secondary stroke prevention — dual antiplatelet therapy
medications.flag: Continue indefinitely. Part of DAPT regimen.

medications.name: Clopidogrel
medications.indication: Secondary stroke prevention — dual antiplatelet (DAPT, 21-day course)
medications.flag: 21-day DAPT course per POINT trial protocol. Transition to aspirin monotherapy on 2026-05-06. Must ensure patient and PCP are aware of stop date.

medications.name: Atorvastatin
medications.flag: NEW — high-intensity statin started for stroke secondary prevention. LDL 142 on admission.

medications.name: Amlodipine
medications.flag: NEW. Added for BP control. BP at goal 138/82 currently.

medications.name: Lisinopril
medications.flag: DOSE DOUBLED from 10mg to 20mg. Monitor renal function and K+.

medications.name: Metformin
medications.flag: HELD during acute phase (contrast, hemodynamic instability). Restarted Day 6. Confirm eGFR adequate.

hba1c.flag: H — above target, DM management optimization needed at follow-up

neuro_imaging.mri_brain: Right MCA territory ischemic infarct, cortical and subcortical, no hemorrhagic transformation. Estimated volume 18 mL.
neuro_imaging.echo: EF 58%, no LV thrombus, no significant valvular disease. Patent foramen ovale identified (small, no right-to-left shunting at rest).
neuro_imaging.carotid_doppler: No significant stenosis bilaterally (<50% on right, <40% on left)

nihss.interpretation: Significant improvement. Mild residual left arm weakness and mild dysmetria.

social.living_situation: Home with wife (Patricia Jackson, 62, retired). Single-story ranch house. No stairs to navigate.
social.primary_support: Wife — retired, committed caregiver, present at all family meetings
social.insurance_type: Medicare Advantage HMO (retired municipal worker plan)

insurance_details.inpatient_rehab: Covered up to 20 days following 3-day qualifying hospital stay (qualified).
insurance_details.skilled_pt_outpatient: 20 visits per calendar year total (PT + OT combined).
insurance_details.note: If inpatient rehab used, outpatient PT visits still count against annual limit.

social.health_literacy: High — engaged, asks clarifying questions, researched stroke recovery online

ambulation.current: Ambulates 50 feet with single-point cane (SPC), unsteady on uneven surfaces

fall_risk.note: Gait asymmetry, reduced left leg proprioception on exam

dysphagia.screen: PASSED — Bedside swallow evaluation normal. Regular diet, thin liquids.

cognitive.nihss_cognitive: Intact — oriented x4, follows complex commands
cognitive.note: No aphasia. Mild emotional lability (post-stroke affect), improving.

transportation.detail: Wife drives. Car accessible. Home 3.2 miles from hospital.

food_security.detail: No concerns. Couple cooks at home.

housing_stability.detail: Single-story ranch — no stairs to navigate. Owned outright. No housing barriers.

medication_affordability.detail: Medicare Advantage covers most costs. Atorvastatin 80mg is generic, low cost. Clopidogrel may have copay — verify.

value_conflict.summary: Three-way conflict between clinical recommendation, patient autonomy, and insurance coverage.

positions.PT_recommendation: Acute inpatient rehabilitation facility (IRF) — patient meets criteria (3+ hours therapy/day tolerated, sufficient cognition, motivated). IRF outcomes for MCA stroke at this severity are significantly better than home PT.
positions.patient_preference: Home immediately. Patient refuses any inpatient facility. States: 'I am not going to a nursing home.' Wife supports patient's decision.
positions.insurance_constraint: Medicare Advantage covers IRF (20 days) — qualifying stay met. However, outpatient PT is capped at 20 visits/year. If patient goes home, 20 visits of outpatient PT is the only rehabilitation available for the rest of 2026.

value_conflict.clinical_stakes: Without adequate rehabilitation, patient at significant risk of permanent functional deficit in left arm. 20 outpatient PT visits may be insufficient for MCA stroke recovery. Re-injury and fall risk elevated at home with current functional status.

discharge_target.current_plan: PENDING — goals of care conversation with patient and wife required
barriers: Patient refusing inpatient rehabilitation despite clinical recommendation
If home: only 20 outpatient PT visits available for calendar year 2026
Left arm motor recovery trajectory uncertain without intensive rehab
Clopidogrel DAPT stop date (2026-05-06) — must be communicated to PCP clearly
pending_decisions: Goals of care conversation: patient/wife must understand functional consequences of refusing IRF
If home: PFO management — cardiology/neurology follow-up for closure discussion
HbA1c 8.1% — endocrinology or PCP to optimize DM management
Driving — patient is retired firefighter who drives. Must not drive until cleared by neurology (typically 3–6 months post-stroke in MA).

follow_up.pt_ot: As soon as possible — every day without therapy is functional decline
